clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Standardized management of hypertension in pregnant individuals to minimize maternal and fetal risk
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Preconception counseling, pregnancy diagnosis, antepartum care, acute hypertension in pregnancy
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Antihypertensive agent selection + BP targets + aspirin prophylaxis + avoidance of harmful medications

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for pregnancy hypertension management
Determine if planning pregnancy, currently pregnant, or postpartum
Determine weeks of gestation (<20 weeks vs ≥20 weeks)
Select antihypertensive agent based on recommendations
Display BP goals based on clinical scenario

rules: Rule 1: Preconception/pregnant → preferred agents: labetalol or extended-release nifedipine (COR 1, LOE A)
Rule 2: Preconception/pregnant → counsel on low-dose aspirin 81 mg/day (COR 1, LOE B-R)
Rule 3: SBP ≥160 OR DBP ≥110 confirmed within 15 min → treat to <160/<110 within 30-60 min (COR 1, LOE B-R)
Rule 4: Chronic hypertension (pre-pregnancy or SBP 140-159/DBP 90-109 before 20 weeks) → target BP <140/90 (COR 1, LOE B-R)
Rule 5: NEVER use in pregnancy: atenolol, ACEi, ARB, direct renin inhibitors, nitroprusside, MRA (COR 3: Harm, LOE C-LD)

category.description: e.g., 'Chronic HTN in Pregnancy', 'Severe HTN in Pregnancy'

symptomsToAsk: Upper abdominal pain (especially right upper quadrant)

escalationLogic.ifAnySymptomPresent: Immediate hospital transfer for possible preeclampsia/eclampsia
escalationLogic.ifNoSymptoms: Repeat BP within 15 minutes; treat with IV/oral antihypertensive to target <160/110 within 30-60 minutes

appDisplay.screenType: Urgent alert screen (orange/yellow for pregnancy-specific crisis)
appDisplay.behavior: Do not delay treatment; initiate protocol within 15 minutes

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Obstetrician or maternal-fetal medicine specialist involvement for BP ≥140/90
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Weekly BP monitoring if chronic HTN; daily if severe range
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Confirm severe elevation within 15 minutes before treatment
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Alert OB triage if SBP ≥160 or DBP ≥110
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log BP readings, medication administration, aspirin counseling, and avoided medications to prenatal record
